title: Implement Windows paste backend (SendInput + HWND)

context.goal: On Windows, capture the foreground window before recording starts, then refocus it and synthesize the transcribed text as keyboard input so it lands at the user's cursor.
context.caller_contract: lib.rs do_toggle calls active_window_id() at record start; finish_dictation calls type_text(text, target.as_deref()) after hiding overlay.
context.hwnd_format: Serialize HWND as decimal i64 string (e.g. "12345678"); parse back with str::parse::<isize>().

api_contract.behavior: GetForegroundWindow(); if HWND.0 == 0 return None else Some(decimal string)
if target Some: parse HWND, raise_window, sleep 80ms; then enigo.text(text)

raise_window_strategy.primary: AttachThreadInput(current_tid, target_tid, TRUE) then SetForegroundWindow(hwnd) then DetachThreadInput. This bypasses the foreground lock that blocks plain SetForegroundWindow.
raise_window_strategy.fallback_if_minimized: If IsIconic(hwnd) is TRUE, call ShowWindow(hwnd, SW_RESTORE) before SetForegroundWindow.
raise_window_strategy.no_focus_steal_workaround: Also call AllowSetForegroundWindow(ASFW_ANY) once at app startup (do this in lib.rs setup, gated cfg(windows)) to whitelist the dictate process.

text_injection_strategy.rationale: enigo::Enigo::text() uses SendInput with KEYEVENTF_UNICODE per char. Handles non-ASCII (ñ, é, 中, emoji), avoids dead-key/IME issues, no need for VkKeyScan.

steps: Replace stub paste/windows.rs with full impl using windows crate + enigo
Implement raise_window(hwnd) using AttachThreadInput strategy + iconic fallback
Implement type_text: short-circuit on empty, raise window if target Some, sleep 80ms, enigo.text(text)
In lib.rs setup() add cfg(windows) block calling AllowSetForegroundWindow(ASFW_ANY = 0xFFFFFFFF). Use ASFW_ANY constant from windows crate.
Build with `cargo build --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc` (on Windows host or via cross)
Manual smoke test on Windows: see T10
edge_cases: Empty text -> return Ok(()) immediately (mirror Linux behavior)
HWND parse failure -> log eprintln but still attempt enigo.text without raise
SetForegroundWindow returns BOOL(0) -> log warning, continue with type anyway
Long text (>2000 chars) -> enigo handles batching; no chunking needed
Target window closed between capture and paste -> raise fails silently, text goes to whatever is foreground now
acceptance: Records audio in Window A, focuses Window B during transcription, text appears in Window A
Unicode chars (test string: "ñ é 中 hello") paste correctly into Notepad, Chrome address bar, VS Code
No UAC prompt or admin requirement at runtime
Compiles with no unused-import warnings on Windows target
Linux build still passes (cfg gating tight)
files_touched: src-tauri/src/lib.rs (add AllowSetForegroundWindow call in setup, cfg(windows))
